name: Law No. 058/2021 Relating to the Protection of Personal Data and Privacy
summary: Rwanda's data protection law regulates the processing of personal data of individuals in Rwanda by both domestic and foreign entities. It included a 24-month compliance period with enforcement beginning from October 2023, requiring data processor registration, DPO appointment, and breach notification.
keyRequirements: Register with the NCSA and obtain a Data Protection and Privacy certificate
Designate a Data Protection Officer (internal or external)
Obtain consent before processing personal data
Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures
Notify the authority of data breaches within 48 hours
Conduct data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing
Restrict cross-border transfers to countries with adequate protection
penalties: Administrative fines from RWF 2,000,000 to RWF 5,000,000 or 1% of global turnover. Criminal penalties for serious violations: 7-10 years imprisonment and fines of RWF 20,000,000-25,000,000. Enforced by the National Cyber Security Authority (NCSA).
